name: Guides: Internationalization | Next.js
snippet: Next.js has built-in support for internationalized (i18n) routing since v10.0.0. You can provide a list of locales, the default locale, and domain-specific locales and Next.js will automatically handle the routing.

name: A Complete Guide to i18n in Next.js 15 App Router with next
snippet: This article walks through implementing multi-language support in Next.js 15 using next-intl, with real production code from DevType - a typing practice game for programmers.

name: Routing configuration – Internationalization (i18n) for Next.js
snippet: In case you’re building an app where locales can be added and removed at runtime, you can provide the routing configuration for the middleware dynamically per request.

name: GitHub - i18nexus/next-i18n-router: Next.js App Router
snippet: This library adds back internationalized routing in addition to locale detection and optional cookie usage to set a user's preferred language. This library can be used with any of the popular Javascript i18n libraries such as react-i18next and react-intl. Tutorials and examples can be found here.

name: Next.js 15 i18n Guide – Build Multi-Language Sites Fast
snippet: Learn how to create blazing-fast, SEO-ready multi-language websites with Next.js 15’s App Router, server-side translations, middleware language detection, and...

name: next-intl Guide: Add i18n to Next.js 16 (Complete Setup)
snippet: Complete next-intl v4 setup for Next.js 16: routing, middleware, setRequestLocale explained, static generation, language switcher + TypeScript. Production-ready i18n in one guide.

name: Next.js 15 App Router Internationalization with URL-Based Routing
snippet: Building internationalization for Next.js 15 App Router requires more manual setup than the old Pages Router, but the result is a more flexible and maintainable solution.
